title: Iraq squad announcement | FIFA World Cup 2026
original_text:
Graham Arnold has named his 26-strong squad for Iraq’s second FIFA World Cup outing, including returning captain Jalal Hassan and the highly-rated Ahmed Qasem.
Less than a fortnight until they make their return to the FIFA World Cup™ after a 40-year absence, head coach Graham Arnold has named his 26-player Iraq squad.
Having named an initial 34-strong squad, the former Australia coach trimmed that selection to the final 26 after a 1-0 win against Andorra on 30 May. The Lions of Mesopotamia are now preparing to face Luis de la Fuente’s Spain side on 4 June.
While veteran goalkeeper Jalal Hassan has returned from injury to make the cut, there was no room for highly-touted Austrian-based midfielder Jussef Nasrawe in the final selection.
Explore hospitality packages for Iraq's FIFA World Cup 2026™ matchesExplore hospitality packages for Iraq's FIFA World Cup 2026™ matches
Iraq's preliminary World Cup squadIraq's preliminary World Cup squad
GoalkeepersGoalkeepers
Ahmed Basil Jalal Hassan Fahad Talib
DefendersDefenders
Hussein Ali Merchas Doski Akam Hashem Frans Putros Mustafa Saadoon Rebin Sulaka Zaid Tahseen Ahmed Maknazi Manaf Younis
MidfieldersMidfielders
Amir Al Ammari Youssef Amyn Ibrahim Bayesh Marko Farji Zidane Iqbal Zaid Ismail Ali Jassim Ahmed Qasim Aimar Sher Kevin Yakob
ForwardsForwards
Mohanad Ali Aymen Hussein Ali Al Hamadi Ali Yousef
All squads are provisional until the final list of 26 players is announced by FIFA, following their submission by participating teams, on 2 June.
Hassan returns and Qasem makes the cut Hassan returns and Qasem makes the cut
The squad is headlined by midfield string puller Amir Al Ammari, creative winger Ali Jasim and the vastly experienced goal plunderer Aymen Hussein. Captain Jalal Hassan also returns, having missed the team’s FIFA Play-Off Tournament win over Bolivia due to injury.
Highly touted winger Montader Madjed misses out, as does 19-year-old Bayern Munich youth product Nasrawe and 20-year-old fullback Dario Naamo.
Previously uncapped, former Sweden youth international Ahmed Qasem has though made the final cut, with the attacking midfielder aiming to feature in the land where he plays his club football with Nashville FC.
Iraq's World Cup 2026 fixtures and groupIraq's World Cup 2026 fixtures and group
16 June: Iraq v Norway - Boston Stadium
22 June: France v Iraq - Philadelphia Stadium
26 June: Senegal v Iraq - Toronto Stadium
